224	    def update_penalties(self):
225	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
226	            self.acc_linear_penalties = torch.zeros(
227	                (len(self.temperatures), self.vocab_size),
228	                dtype=torch.float32,
229	                device=self.temperatures.device,
230	            )
231	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(self.acc_linear_penalties)
232	        else:
233	            self.acc_linear_penalties = None
234	
235	    def apply_logits_bias(self, logits: torch.Tensor):
236	        if self.acc_linear_penalties is not None:
237	            # Used in the overlap mode
238	            logits.add_(self.acc_linear_penalties)
239	
240	        if self.penalizer_orchestrator and self.penalizer_orchestrator.is_required:
241	            # Used in the non-overlap mode
242	            self.penalizer_orchestrator.apply(logits)
243	
244	        if self.vocab_mask is not None:
245	            self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
246	
247	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
248	            logits.add_(self.logit_bias)
249	
250	    def filter_batch(self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor):
251	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.filter(keep_indices_device)
252	
253	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor:
254	            self._filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(keep_indices, keep_indices_device)
255	
256	        for item in [
257	            "temperatures",
258	            "top_ps",
259	            "top_ks",
260	            "min_ps",
261	            "sampling_seed",
262	        ]:
263	            value = getattr(self, item, None)
264	            if value is not None:
265	                setattr(self, item, value[keep_indices_device])
266	
267	        if self.logit_bias is not None:
268	            self.logit_bias = self.logit_bias[keep_indices_device]
269	
270	    def _filter_batch_custom_logit_processor(
271	        self, keep_indices: List[int], keep_indices_device: torch.Tensor
272	    ):
273	        """Filter the custom logit processor and custom params"""
274	        self.custom_logit_processor = {
275	            k: (p, mask[keep_indices_device])
276	            for k, (p, mask) in self.custom_logit_processor.items()
277	            if torch.any(
278	                mask[keep_indices_device]
279	            )  # ignore the custom logit processor whose mask is all False
280	        }
281	        self.custom_params = [self.custom_params[i] for i in keep_indices]
282	
283	        # If the custom logit processor is an empty dict, set the flag to False,
284	        # and set the custom logit processor and custom params to None.
285	        if len(self.custom_logit_processor) == 0:
286	            self.custom_logit_processor = None
287	            self.custom_params = None
288	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = False
289	
290	    @staticmethod
291	    def merge_custom_logit_processor(
292	        lhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
293	        rhs: Optional[Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]],
294	        bs1: int,
295	        bs2: int,
296	        device: str,
297	    ):
298	        if lhs is None and rhs is None:
299	            return None
300	        lhs, rhs = lhs or {}, rhs or {}
301	
302	        keys = set(lhs.keys()).union(set(rhs.keys()))
303	        merged_dict = {}
304	
305	        for k in keys:
306	            # Get the logit processor object
307	            processor = lhs[k][0] if k in lhs else rhs[k][0]
308	            # Get and merge the mask tensors from the two dicts
309	            left_mask = (
310	                lhs[k][1]
311	                if k in lhs
312	                else torch.zeros(bs1, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
313	            )
314	            right_mask = (
315	                rhs[k][1]
316	                if k in rhs
317	                else torch.zeros(bs2, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
318	            )
319	            merged_dict[k] = (processor, torch.cat([left_mask, right_mask]))
320	
321	            assert merged_dict[k][1].shape[0] == bs1 + bs2, (
322	                f"The batch size of merged mask ({merged_dict[k][1].shape[0]}) does not match "
323	                f"the sum of the batch sizes of the two masks ({bs1 + bs2})"
324	                f"\n{left_mask=}\n{right_mask=}\n{bs1=}\n{bs2=}"
325	                f"\n{lhs=}\n{rhs=}"
326	            )
327	
328	        return merged_dict
329	
330	    def merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
331	        self.penalizer_orchestrator.merge(other.penalizer_orchestrator)
332	
333	        # Merge the custom logit processors and custom params lists
334	        if self.has_custom_logit_processor or other.has_custom_logit_processor:
335	            # Merge the custom logit processors
336	            self.custom_logit_processor = (
337	                SamplingBatchInfo.merge_custom_logit_processor(
338	                    self.custom_logit_processor,
339	                    other.custom_logit_processor,
340	                    len(self),
341	                    len(other),
342	                    self.device,
343	                )
344	            )
345	            # Merge the custom params lists
346	            self.custom_params = self.custom_params or [None] * len(self)
347	            other.custom_params = other.custom_params or [None] * len(other)
348	            self.custom_params.extend(other.custom_params)
349	
350	            # Set the flag to True if any of the two has custom logit processor
351	            self.has_custom_logit_processor = True
352	
353	        # Merge logit bias - note this has to come before the temperatures tensor update! Otherwise will cause crashes.
354	        # See note below on len(self) and len(other).
355	        self.logit_bias = merge_bias_tensor(
356	            self.logit_bias, other.logit_bias, len(self), len(other), self.device, 0.0
357	        )
358	
359	        # Note: because the __len()__ operator is defined on the temperatures tensor,
360	        # please make sure any merge operation with len(self) or len(other) is done before
361	        # the merge operation of the temperatures tensor below.
362	        for item in [
363	            "temperatures",
364	            "top_ps",
365	            "top_ks",
366	            "min_ps",
367	            "sampling_seed",
368	        ]:
369	            self_val = getattr(self, item, None)
370	            other_val = getattr(other, item, None)
371	            if self_val is not None and other_val is not None:
372	                setattr(self, item, torch.cat([self_val, other_val]))
373	
374	        self.is_all_greedy &= other.is_all_greedy
375	        self.need_top_p_sampling |= other.need_top_p_sampling
376	        self.need_top_k_sampling |= other.need_top_k_sampling
377	        self.need_min_p_sampling |= other.need_min_p_sampling
378	
379	        self.adjusted_merge_batch(other)
380	
381	    def copy_for_forward(self):
382	        # Accumulate the penalty into a pre-allocated buffer to get rid of the dependency of `penalizer_orchestrator` later
383	        self.update_penalties()
384	        return dataclasses.replace(self, penalizer_orchestrator=None)
385	
386	
387	def merge_bias_tensor(
388	    lhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
389	    rhs: Optional[torch.Tensor],
390	    bs1: int,
391	    bs2: int,
392	    device: str,
393	    default: float,
394	):
395	    """Merge two bias tensors for batch merging.
396	
397	    Args:
398	        lhs: Left-hand side tensor
399	        rhs: Right-hand side tensor
400	        bs1: Batch size of left-hand side tensor
401	        bs2: Batch size of right-hand side tensor
402	        device: Device to place the merged tensor on
403	        default: Default value for missing tensor elements
404	
405	    Returns:
406	        Merged tensor or None if both inputs are None
407	    """
408	    if lhs is None and rhs is None:
409	        return None
410	
411	    if lhs is not None and rhs is not None:
412	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
413	    else:
414	        if lhs is not None:
415	            shape, dtype = lhs.shape[1:], lhs.dtype
416	        else:
417	            shape, dtype = rhs.shape[1:], rhs.dtype
418	
419	        if lhs is None:
420	            lhs = torch.empty((bs1, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
421	        if rhs is None:
422	            rhs = torch.empty((bs2, *shape), device=device, dtype=dtype).fill_(default)
423	        return torch.cat([lhs, rhs])
424

8	class TestJSONConstrainedMixin:
9	    json_schema = json.dumps(
10	        {

13	                "name": {"type": "string", "pattern": "^[\\w]+$"},
14	                "population": {"type": "integer"},
15	            },
16	            "required": ["name", "population"],
17	            "additionalProperties": False,
18	        }
19	    )
20	
21	    def _run_decode_json(
22	        self, json_schema, return_logprob=False, top_logprobs_num=0, n=1
23	    ):
24	        response = requests.post(
25	            self.base_url + "/generate",
26	            json={
27	                "text": (
28	                    "Introduce the capital of France. Return in a JSON format. The JSON Schema is: "
29	                    + json.dumps(json_schema)
30	                ),
31	                "sampling_params": {
32	                    "temperature": 0 if n == 1 else 0.5,
33	                    "max_new_tokens": 128,
34	                    "n": n,
35	                    "stop_token_ids": [119690],
36	                    "json_schema": json_schema,
37	                },
38	                "stream": False,
39	                "return_logprob": return_logprob,
40	                "top_logprobs_num": top_logprobs_num,
41	                "logprob_start_len": 0,
42	            },
43	        )
44	        ret = response.json()
45	        print(json.dumps(ret))
46	        print("=" * 100)
47	
48	        if not json_schema or json_schema == "INVALID":
49	            return
50	
51	        # Make sure the json output is valid
52	        try:
53	            js_obj = json.loads(ret["text"])
54	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
55	            raise
56	
57	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
58	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
59	
60	    def test_json_generate(self):
61	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema=self.json_schema)

63	    def test_json_invalid(self):
64	        self._run_decode_json(json_schema="INVALID")
65	
66	    def test_json_openai(self):
67	        client = openai.Client(api_key="EMPTY", base_url=f"{self.base_url}/v1")
68	
69	        response = client.chat.completions.create(
70	            model=self.model,

72	                {"role": "system", "content": "You are a helpful AI assistant"},
73	                {
74	                    "role": "user",

76	                    "The JSON Schema is: " + json.dumps(self.json_schema),
77	                },
78	            ],
79	            temperature=0,
80	            max_tokens=128,
81	            response_format={
82	                "type": "json_schema",
83	                "json_schema": {"name": "foo", "schema": json.loads(self.json_schema)},
84	            },
85	        )
86	        text = response.choices[0].message.content
87	
88	        try:
89	            js_obj = json.loads(text)
90	        except (TypeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError):
91	            print("JSONDecodeError", text)

93	
94	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["name"], str)
95	        self.assertIsInstance(js_obj["population"], int)
96	
97	    def test_mix_json_and_other(self):
98	        json_schemas = [None, None, self.json_schema, self.json_schema] * 10
99	
100	        with ThreadPoolExecutor(len(json_schemas)) as executor:
101	            list(executor.map(self._run_decode_json, json_schemas))

53	class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
54	
55	    def __init__(
56	        self,
57	        matcher: GrammarMatcher,
58	        vocab_size: int,
59	        ctx: CompiledGrammar,
60	        override_stop_tokens: Optional[Union[List[int], int]],
61	        key_string: Optional[str] = None,  # TODO (sk): for debugging, remove later
62	        grammar_stats: Optional[GrammarStats] = GrammarStats(),
63	    ) -> None:
64	        super().__init__()
65	        self.matcher = matcher
66	        self.vocab_size = vocab_size
67	        self.ctx = ctx
68	        self.override_stop_tokens = override_stop_tokens
69	        self.accepted_tokens = []
70	        self.key_string = key_string
71	        self.grammar_stats = grammar_stats
72	
73	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
74	        if not self.is_terminated():
75	            self.current_token = token
76	            accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
77	            if not accepted:
78	                # log for debugging
79	                raise ValueError(
80	                    f"Tokens not accepted: {token}\n"
81	                    f"Accepted tokens: {self.accepted_tokens}\n"
82	                    f"Key string: {self.key_string}"
83	                )
84	            else:
85	                self.accepted_tokens.append(token)
86	
87	    def rollback(self, k: int):
88	        self.matcher.rollback(k)
89	        self.accepted_tokens = self.accepted_tokens[:-k]
90	
91	    def is_terminated(self):
92	        return self.matcher.is_terminated()
93	
94	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
95	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
96	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
97	        return allocate_token_bitmask(batch_size, vocab_size)
98	
99	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
100	        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)

42	class OutlinesGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
43	    def __init__(
44	        self,
45	        guide: RegexGuide,
46	        jump_forward_map: Union[OutlinesJumpForwardMap, None],
47	    ) -> None:
48	        super().__init__()
49	        self.guide = guide
50	        self.jump_forward_map = jump_forward_map
51	        self.state = 0
52	
53	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
54	        self.state = self.guide.get_next_state(self.state, token)
55	
56	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
57	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
58	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
59	        return torch.zeros(batch_size, vocab_size, dtype=torch.bool, device=device)
60	
61	    @staticmethod
62	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
63	        return vocab_mask
64	
65	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
66	        tokens = torch.tensor(
67	            self.guide.get_next_instruction(self.state).tokens, dtype=torch.int64
68	        ).to(vocab_mask.device, non_blocking=True)
69	        vocab_mask = vocab_mask[idx]
70	        vocab_mask.fill_(1)
71	        vocab_mask.scatter_(0, tokens, torch.zeros_like(tokens, dtype=torch.bool))
72	
73	    @staticmethod
74	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor):
75	        logits.masked_fill_(vocab_mask, float("-inf"))
76	
77	    def copy(self):
78	        return OutlinesGrammar(self.guide, self.jump_forward_map)
79	
80	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple]:
81	        if not self.jump_forward_map:
82	            return None
83	
84	        jump_forward_bytes = self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_byte(self.state)
85	        if jump_forward_bytes is None or len(jump_forward_bytes) <= 1:
86	            return None
87	
88	        # preprocess the jump forward string
89	        suffix_bytes = []
90	        continuation_range = range(0x80, 0xC0)
91	        cur_state = self.state
92	        while (
93	            len(jump_forward_bytes) and jump_forward_bytes[0][0] in continuation_range
94	        ):
95	            # continuation bytes
96	            byte_edge = jump_forward_bytes.pop(0)
97	            suffix_bytes.append(byte_edge[0])
98	            cur_state = byte_edge[1]
99	
100	        suffix_tokens = [f"<0x{hex(b)[2:].upper()}>" for b in suffix_bytes]
101	        suffix_ids = tokenizer.convert_tokens_to_ids(suffix_tokens)
102	        return suffix_ids, cur_state
103	
104	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
105	        _, cur_state = helper
106	        return self.jump_forward_map.jump_forward_symbol(cur_state)
107	
108	    def jump_and_retokenize(
109	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
110	    ):
111	        self.state = next_state
112	
113	
114	class OutlinesGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
115	    def __init__(
116	        self,
117	        tokenizer,
118	        whitespace_pattern: str | None,
119	    ):
120	        super().__init__()
121	
122	        try:
123	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
124	        except AttributeError:
125	            # FIXME: tmp fix for chatglm2 & chatglm3 (pad_token_id=0)
126	            origin_pad_token_id = tokenizer.pad_token_id
127	
128	            def fset(self, value):
129	                self._value = value
130	
131	            type(tokenizer).pad_token_id = property(
132	                fget=type(tokenizer).pad_token_id.fget, fset=fset
133	            )
134	            self.outlines_tokenizer = TransformerTokenizer(tokenizer)
135	            self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
136	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token_id = origin_pad_token_id
137	            self.outlines_tokenizer.pad_token = (
138	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.pad_token
139	            )
140	            self.outlines_tokenizer.vocabulary = (
141	                self.outlines_tokenizer.tokenizer.get_vocab()
142	            )
143	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
144	
145	    def _compile_regex(self, regex: str) -> Optional[OutlinesGrammar]:
146	        try:
147	            if hasattr(RegexGuide, "from_regex"):
148	                # outlines >= 0.1.1
149	                guide = RegexGuide.from_regex(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
150	            else:
151	                # outlines <= 0.0.46
152	                guide = RegexGuide(regex, self.outlines_tokenizer)
153	        except interegular.patterns.InvalidSyntax as e:
154	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex schema: {regex=}, {e=}")
155	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
156	
157	        jump_forward_map = None
158	        return OutlinesGrammar(guide, jump_forward_map)
159	
160	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str):
161	        return super().dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
162	
163	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str):
164	        return super().dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
165	
166	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str):
167	        try:
168	            regex = build_regex_from_object(
169	                key_string,
170	                whitespace_pattern=self.whitespace_pattern,
171	            )
172	        except (NotImplementedError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, ValueError) as e:
173	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
174	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
175	        return self._compile_regex(regex)
176	
177	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str):
178	        return self._compile_regex(key_string)
179	
180	
181	def build_regex_from_object(
182	    object: Union[str, BaseModel, Dict], whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None
183	):
184	    if isinstance(object, type(BaseModel)):
185	        schema = json.dumps(object.model_json_schema())
186	    elif isinstance(object, Dict):
187	        schema = json.dumps(object)
188	    else:
189	        schema = object
190	    return build_regex_from_schema(schema, whitespace_pattern)
191

257	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
258	        try:
259	            if key_string == "$$ANY$$":
260	                # Note: This builtin JSON grammar includes *all* valid JSON (including, for example, arrays at the root)
261	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_builtin_json_grammar()
262	            else:
263	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(
264	                    schema=key_string, any_whitespace=self.any_whitespace
265	                )
266	
267	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError, UnicodeDecodeError) as e:
268	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
269	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
270	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
271	
272	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
273	        try:
274	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
275	        except RuntimeError as e:
276	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
277	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
278	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
279	
280	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
281	        try:
282	            ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
283	        except RuntimeError as e:
284	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid regex: {key_string=}, {e=}")
285	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
286	        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
287	
288	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
289	        try:
290	            # TODO(dark): it's REALLY stupid to construct object from string and decode it again
291	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
292	            if is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag):
293	                self._sanitize_structural_tag_structures(structural_tag)
294	                tags = [
295	                    StructuralTagItem(
296	                        begin=structure["begin"],
297	                        schema=json.dumps(structure["schema"]),
298	                        end=structure["end"],
299	                    )
300	                    for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
301	                ]
302	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(
303	                    tags, structural_tag["triggers"]
304	                )
305	            else:
306	                format_dict = structural_tag.get("format")
307	                if isinstance(format_dict, dict):
308	                    self._sanitize_structural_format(format_dict)
309	                    structural_tag["format"] = format_dict
310	                    key_string = json.dumps(structural_tag)
311	                ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_structural_tag(key_string)
312	        except (RuntimeError, json.decoder.JSONDecodeError) as e:
313	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
314	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
315	        return self._from_context(
316	            ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="structural_tag")
317	        )

320	        self.grammar_compiler.clear_cache()

332	    vocab_size = hf_config.vocab_size

334	
335	    backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
336	        tokenizer, vocab_size=vocab_size, model_eos_token_ids=[eos_token_id]
337	    )
338	    regex = r"hello (world|there)"
339	    grammar = backend.dispatch_regex(regex)
340	    tokens = [
341	        tokenizer.encode(t, add_special_tokens=False)[0] for t in ["hello", " world"]
342	    ]
343	
344	    # Test termination
345	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[0])  # accept "hello"
346	    grammar.accept_token(tokens[1])  # accept " world"
347	    grammar.accept_token(eos_token_id)  # accept EOS
348	    assert grammar.is_terminated()
349	
350	    # Test rollback the terminated state
351	    grammar.rollback(1)
352	    assert not grammar.is_terminated()
353	
354	
355	if __name__ == "__main__":
356	    demo_test()

14	"""Constrained decoding with llguidance backend."""
15	
16	import json
17	import logging
18	import os
19	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
20	
21	import torch
22	from llguidance import LLMatcher, LLTokenizer, StructTag, grammar_from
23	from llguidance.hf import from_tokenizer
24	from llguidance.torch import (
25	    allocate_token_bitmask,
26	    apply_token_bitmask_inplace,
27	    fill_next_token_bitmask,
28	)
29	
30	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
31	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
32	    BaseGrammarBackend,
33	    BaseGrammarObject,
34	)
35	from sglang.srt.constrained.utils import is_legacy_structural_tag
36	
37	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
38	
39	
40	class GuidanceGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
41	
42	    def __init__(self, llguidance_tokenizer: LLTokenizer, serialized_grammar: str):
43	        super().__init__()
44	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = llguidance_tokenizer
45	        self.serialized_grammar = serialized_grammar
46	
47	        self.ll_matcher = LLMatcher(
48	            self.llguidance_tokenizer,
49	            self.serialized_grammar,
50	            log_level=int(os.environ.get("LLGUIDANCE_LOG_LEVEL", "1")),
51	        )
52	        self.bitmask = None
53	
54	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
55	        if not self.ll_matcher.consume_token(token):
56	            logger.warning(f"matcher error: {self.ll_matcher.get_error()}")
57	            self.finished = True
58	
59	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
60	        if self.ll_matcher.is_stopped():
61	            self.finished = True
62	
63	        fill_next_token_bitmask(self.ll_matcher, vocab_mask, idx)
64	
65	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
66	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
67	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
68	        if self.bitmask is None or self.bitmask.shape[0] < batch_size:
69	            # only create bitmask when batch gets larger
70	            self.bitmask = allocate_token_bitmask(
71	                batch_size, self.llguidance_tokenizer.vocab_size
72	            )
73	            bitmask = self.bitmask
74	        else:
75	            bitmask = self.bitmask[:batch_size]
76	
77	        return bitmask
78	
79	    @staticmethod
80	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
81	        return vocab_mask.to(device, non_blocking=True)
82	
83	    @staticmethod
84	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
85	        apply_token_bitmask_inplace(logits, vocab_mask)
86	
87	    def copy(self):
88	        return GuidanceGrammar(
89	            llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
90	            serialized_grammar=self.serialized_grammar,
91	        )
92	
93	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
94	        ff_tokens = self.ll_matcher.compute_ff_tokens()
95	        if ff_tokens:
96	            return ff_tokens, ""
97	        else:
98	            return None
99	
100	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
101	        return "", -1
102	
103	    def jump_and_retokenize(
104	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
105	    ):
106	        pass
107	
108	
109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)
151	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
152	
153	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
154	        try:
155	            serialized_grammar = grammar_from("ebnf", key_string)
156	            return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
157	        except ValueError as e:
158	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid ebnf: {key_string=}, {e=}")
159	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
160	
161	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
162	        try:
163	            structural_tag = json.loads(key_string)
164	            assert is_legacy_structural_tag(structural_tag)
165	            tags = [
166	                StructTag(
167	                    begin=structure["begin"],
168	                    grammar=structure["schema"],
169	                    end=structure["end"],
170	                    trigger=structural_tag["triggers"][0],  # TODO?
171	                )
172	                for structure in structural_tag["structures"]
173	            ]
174	            g = StructTag.to_grammar(tags)
175	            return self._from_serialized(g)
176	        except Exception as e:
177	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid structural_tag: {key_string=}, {e=}")
178	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
179

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for reasoner grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	from typing import List, Optional, Tuple
17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
25	
26	
27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67	
68	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask
83	
84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):
99	        return self.grammar.jump_forward_str_state(helper)
100	
101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):
104	        return self.grammar.jump_and_retokenize(
105	            old_output_ids, new_output_ids, next_state
106	        )
107	
108	
109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
114	
115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj
125

68	@dataclass
69	class RequestTimingMetricsMixin:
70	    """
71	    Mixin class containing common request-level timing metrics.
72	
73	    This class consolidates the timing metrics that are shared across all batch output types
74	    to avoid code duplication and ensure consistency.
75	    """
76	
77	    # Queue duration: time spent waiting in queue before request is scheduled.
78	    queue_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
79	
80	    # Forward entry time: timestamp when the request enters the forward pass stage.
81	    # This corresponds to `forward_entry_time` in TimeStats.
82	    # In different modes:
83	    #   - Unified/PD-colocate: timestamp when forward computation begins (covers prefill + decode)
84	    #   - Prefill instance (P): timestamp when prefill forward pass begins
85	    #   - Decode instance (D): timestamp when decode forward pass begins
86	    # Note: This is NOT the same as prefill_start_time. There may be a delay between
87	    # forward_entry_time and prefill_start_time (see prefill_launch_delay).
88	    forward_entry_time: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
89	
90	    # Prefill launch delay: time spent waiting between forward entry and prefill start.
91	    # Calculated as: prefill_start_time - forward_entry_time
92	    # This represents the delay between when the request enters the forward stage
93	    # and when prefill computation actually begins.
94	    prefill_launch_delay: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
95	
96	    # Prefill launch latency: time spent during prefill kernel launch.
97	    # Calculated as: prefill_end_time_host - prefill_start_time_host
98	    prefill_launch_latency: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
99	
100	    # Prefill finished time: timestamp when prefill phase completes (wall clock time).
101	    # This marks when the prefill computation finishes.
102	    prefill_finished_ts: Optional[List[Optional[float]]]
103	
104	
105	@dataclass
106	class SpeculativeDecodingMetricsMixin:
107	    """
108	    Mixin class containing speculative decoding metrics.
109	
110	    This class consolidates speculative decoding metrics that are shared across
111	    batch output types that support speculative decoding to avoid code duplication.
112	    """
113	
114	    # Verify count: number of verification forward passes
115	    spec_verify_ct: List[int]
116	
117	    # Accepted tokens: Number of accepted tokens during speculative decoding
118	    spec_accepted_tokens: List[int]
119	
120	    # Acceptance histogram: List of lists, where each inner list represents histogram counts.
121	    # List index = number of accepted tokens in a step, List value = count of steps with that many accepted tokens.
122	    # Example: histogram[0] = 5 means 5 steps with 0 accepted tokens, histogram[3] = 10 means 10 steps with 3 accepted tokens.
123	    # Empty list [] when speculative decoding is disabled.
124	    spec_acceptance_histogram: List[List[int]]
125

549	        # Input and output info
550	        self.rid = rid
551	        self.origin_input_text = origin_input_text
552	        self.origin_input_ids_unpadded = (
553	            origin_input_ids_unpadded

557	        self.origin_input_ids = origin_input_ids

565	        # For req-level memory management

568	        self.kv_committed_freed = False

570	
571	        # for corss-endoder model
572	        self.token_type_ids = token_type_ids
573	
574	        # The length of KV that have been removed in swa cache.
575	        # SWA KV cache eviction behavior differs by cache type:
576	        # - Radix cache: KV in range [cache_protected_len, swa_evicted_seqlen) is freed manually in
577	        #   `ScheduleBatch.maybe_evict_swa`; KV in range [0, cache_protected_len) is freed during radix cache eviction.
578	        # - Chunk cache: KV in range [0, swa_evicted_seqlen) is freed manually in `ScheduleBatch.maybe_evict_swa`.
579	        self.swa_evicted_seqlen = 0
580	
581	        # The index of the extend / decode batch
582	        self.extend_batch_idx = 0
583	        self.decode_batch_idx = 0
584	
585	        # For multi-http worker

587	
588	        # Require reasoning for the request (hybrid reasoning model only)

590	
591	        # Sampling info
592	        if isinstance(sampling_params.custom_params, dict):
593	            sampling_params = copy.copy(sampling_params)
594	            sampling_params.custom_params = sampling_params.custom_params | {

597	        self.sampling_params = sampling_params
598	        self.custom_logit_processor = custom_logit_processor
599	        self.return_hidden_states = return_hidden_states

141	class SglFunction:
142	    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
143	        self.func = func
144	        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
145	        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
146	        self.pin_prefix_rid = None
147	
148	        # Parse arguments
149	        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
150	        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
151	        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
152	        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []
153	
154	    def bind(self, **kwargs):
155	        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)
156	
157	        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
158	        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)
159	
160	    def run(
161	        self,
162	        *args,
163	        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
164	        n: int = 1,
165	        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
166	        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
167	        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
168	        temperature: float = 1.0,
169	        top_p: float = 1.0,
170	        top_k: int = -1,
171	        min_p: float = 0.0,
172	        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
173	        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
174	        ignore_eos: bool = False,
175	        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
176	        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
177	        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
178	        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
179	        stream: bool = False,
180	        backend=None,
181	        use_thread: bool = True,
182	        **kwargs,
183	    ):
184	        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program
185	
186	        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
187	        if stop is None:
188	            stop = []
189	        if stop_token_ids is None:
190	            stop_token_ids = []
191	        if stop_regex is None:
192	            stop_regex = []
193	
194	        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
195	            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
196	            n=n,
197	            stop=stop,
198	            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
199	            stop_regex=stop_regex,
200	            temperature=temperature,
201	            top_p=top_p,
202	            top_k=top_k,
203	            min_p=min_p,
204	            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
205	            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
206	            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
207	            return_logprob=return_logprob,
208	            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
209	            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
210	            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
211	        )
212	        backend = backend or global_config.default_backend
213	        return run_program(
214	            self,
215	            backend,
216	            args,
217	            kwargs,
218	            default_sampling_para,
219	            stream,
220	            use_thread=use_thread,
221	        )

649	@app.api_route("/generate", methods=["POST", "PUT"])
650	async def generate_request(obj: GenerateReqInput, request: Request):
651	    """Handle a generate request."""
652	    if obj.stream:
653	
654	        async def stream_results() -> AsyncIterator[bytes]:
655	            try:
656	                async for out in _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
657	                    obj, request
658	                ):
659	                    yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
660	                        out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
661	                    ) + b"\n\n"
662	            except ValueError as e:
663	                out = {"error": {"message": str(e)}}
664	                logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
665	                yield b"data: " + orjson.dumps(
666	                    out, option=orjson.OPT_NON_STR_KEYS
667	                ) + b"\n\n"
668	            yield b"data: [DONE]\n\n"
669	
670	        return StreamingResponse(
671	            stream_results(),
672	            media_type="text/event-stream",
673	            background=_global_state.tokenizer_manager.create_abort_task(obj),
674	        )
675	    else:
676	        try:
677	            ret = await _global_state.tokenizer_manager.generate_request(
678	                obj, request
679	            ).__anext__()
680	            return ret
681	        except ValueError as e:
682	            logger.error(f"[http_server] Error: {e}")
683	            return _create_error_response(e)

14	character_regex = (
15	    r"""\{\n"""
16	    + r"""    "name": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
17	    + r"""    "house": "(Gryffindor|Slytherin|Ravenclaw|Hufflepuff)",\n"""
18	    + r"""    "blood status": "(Pure-blood|Half-blood|Muggle-born)",\n"""
19	    + r"""    "occupation": "(student|teacher|auror|ministry of magic|death eater|order of the phoenix)",\n"""
20	    + r"""    "wand": \{\n"""
21	    + r"""        "wood": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
22	    + r"""        "core": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
23	    + r"""        "length": [0-9]{1,2}\.[0-9]{0,2}\n"""
24	    + r"""    \},\n"""
25	    + r"""    "alive": "(Alive|Deceased)",\n"""
26	    + r"""    "patronus": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}",\n"""
27	    + r"""    "bogart": "[\w\d\s]{1,16}"\n"""
28	    + r"""\}"""
29	)
30	
31	
32	@sgl.function
33	def character_gen(s, name):
34	    s += (
35	        name
36	        + " is a character in Harry Potter. Please fill in the following information about this character.\n"
37	    )
38	    s += "The constrained regex is:\n"
39	    s += character_regex + "\n"
40	    s += "The JSON output is:\n"
41	    s += sgl.gen("json_output", max_tokens=256, regex=character_regex)
42	
43	
44	def driver_character_gen():
45	    state = character_gen.run(name="Hermione Granger")
46	    print(state.text())

9	    """
10	    Parser for JSON array tool calls when JSON schema constraints are active.
11	
12	    This parser is used when tool_choice="required" or a specific tool is named,
13	    bypassing model-specific parsers in favor of direct JSON array parsing.
14	    """
15	
16	    def __init__(self):
17	        super().__init__()
18	        # Configure for JSON array parsing
19	        self.bot_token = "["
20	        self.eot_token = "]"
21	        self.tool_call_separator = ","
22	
23	    def has_tool_call(self, text: str) -> bool:
24	        """
25	        Check if the given text contains a JSON tool call (array or single object).
26	        """
27	        return "[" in text or "{" in text
28	
29	    def detect_and_parse(self, text: str, tools: List[Tool]) -> StreamingParseResult:
30	        """
31	        Parse JSON tool calls using the base class implementation.
32	        """
33	        raise NotImplementedError(
34	            "Detect and parse not supported for JSON schema constraints."
35	        )
36	
37	    def parse_streaming_increment(
38	        self, new_text: str, tools: List[Tool]
39	    ) -> StreamingParseResult:
40	        """
41	        Streaming incremental parsing with tool validation.
42	        """
43	        return super().parse_streaming_increment(new_text, tools)
44	
45	    def structure_info(self) -> callable:
46	        """
47	        Return a function that creates StructureInfo for constrained generation.
48	        This is not used for JSON schema constraints as they are handled
49	        by the constraint backends directly.
50	        """
51	        raise NotImplementedError("structure_info not used for JSON schema constraints")

61	def _extract_max_dynamic_patch(request: ChatCompletionRequest):
62	    img_vals = []
63	    vid_vals = []
64	    for msg in request.messages or []:
65	        content = getattr(msg, "content", None)
66	        if not isinstance(content, list):
67	            continue
68	        for part in content:
69	            # pydantic object or dict type
70	            if getattr(part, "type", None) == "image_url":
71	                iu = getattr(part, "image_url", None)
72	                mdp = getattr(iu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if iu else None
73	                if mdp is not None:
74	                    img_vals.append(int(mdp))
75	            elif getattr(part, "type", None) == "video_url":
76	                vu = getattr(part, "video_url", None)
77	                mdp = getattr(vu, "max_dynamic_patch", None) if vu else None
78	                if mdp is not None:
79	                    vid_vals.append(int(mdp))
80	
81	    # TODO(yuan-luo): per-item max_dynamic_patch for both image and video
82	    img_max_dynamic_patch = min(img_vals) if img_vals else None
83	    vid_max_dynamic_patch = min(vid_vals) if vid_vals else None
84	    return img_max_dynamic_patch, vid_max_dynamic_patch

92	    def __init__(
93	        self,
94	        tokenizer_manager: TokenizerManager,
95	        template_manager: TemplateManager,
96	    ):
97	        super().__init__(tokenizer_manager)
98	        self.template_manager = template_manager
99	        self.tool_call_parser = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.tool_call_parser
100	        self.reasoning_parser = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.reasoning_parser
101	
102	        # Get default sampling parameters from model's generation config
103	        self.default_sampling_params = (
104	            self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.get_default_sampling_params()
105	        )
106	        if (
107	            self.default_sampling_params
108	            and not OpenAIServingChat._default_sampling_params_logged
109	        ):
110	            logger.info(
111	                f"Using default chat sampling params from model generation config: {self.default_sampling_params}",
112	            )
113	            OpenAIServingChat._default_sampling_params_logged = True
114	
115	        # Check if the model is a GPT-OSS model
116	        self.is_gpt_oss = (
117	            hasattr(self.tokenizer_manager.model_config, "hf_config")
118	            and hasattr(self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config, "model_type")
119	            and self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config.model_type == "gpt_oss"
120	        )
121	
122	        self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding = self._use_dpsk_v32_encoding()

124	    def _handle_last_assistant_message(
125	        self,
126	        messages: List[Dict[str, Any]],
127	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
128	    ) -> tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], Optional[str]]:
129	        """
130	        Handle continue_final_message feature: separate final assistant message.
131	
132	        If continue_final_message is enabled and the last message is from assistant,
133	        extract its content and remove it from the message list.
134	        If continue_final_message is False and the last message is from assistant,
135	        convert it to a user message to ensure the last message is always from user.
136	
137	        Only processes text-based content (strings), ignoring multimodal content (lists).
138	
139	        Args:
140	            messages: List of message dictionaries
141	            request: ChatCompletionRequest with continue_final_message flag
142	
143	        Returns:
144	            Tuple of (processed_messages, assistant_prefix)
145	            - processed_messages: Messages with last assistant message handled appropriately
146	            - assistant_prefix: Content of the last assistant message (string only), or None
147	        """
148	        assistant_prefix = None
149	        if messages and messages[-1].get("role") == "assistant":
150	            last_content = messages[-1].get("content")
151	            # Only process string content, ignore multimodal content (lists)
152	            if isinstance(last_content, str):
153	                if request.continue_final_message:
154	                    # Extract content and remove the assistant message
155	                    assistant_prefix = last_content
156	                    messages = messages[:-1]
157	                else:
158	                    # Convert the last assistant message to user message
159	                    messages[-1] = {"role": "user", "content": last_content}
160	        return messages, assistant_prefix
161	
162	    def _append_assistant_prefix_to_prompt_ids(
163	        self, prompt_ids: List[int], assistant_prefix: str
164	    ) -> List[int]:
165	        """
166	        Append assistant prefix to prompt_ids.
167	
168	        Args:
169	            prompt_ids: Current prompt token IDs
170	            assistant_prefix: Assistant message content to append
171	
172	        Returns:
173	            Updated prompt_ids with assistant prefix appended
174	        """
175	        encoded = self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.encode(assistant_prefix)
176	        if encoded and encoded[0] == self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.bos_token_id:
177	            encoded = encoded[1:]
178	        return prompt_ids + encoded
179	
180	    def _use_dpsk_v32_encoding(self) -> bool:
181	        has_chat_template = (
182	            self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer is not None
183	            and self.tokenizer_manager.tokenizer.chat_template is not None
184	        )
185	        architectures = self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.hf_config.architectures
186	        is_dpsk_v32 = "DeepseekV3" in architectures[0] if architectures else False
187	        return not has_chat_template and is_dpsk_v32
188	
189	    def _request_id_prefix(self) -> str:
190	        return "chatcmpl-"
191	
192	    def _validate_request(self, request: ChatCompletionRequest) -> Optional[str]:
193	        """Validate that the input is valid."""
194	        if not request.messages:
195	            return "Messages cannot be empty."
196	
197	        if (
198	            isinstance(request.tool_choice, str)
199	            and request.tool_choice.lower() == "required"
200	            and not request.tools
201	        ):
202	            return "Tools cannot be empty if tool choice is set to required."
203	
204	        if request.tool_choice is not None and not isinstance(request.tool_choice, str):
205	            if not request.tools:
206	                return "Tools cannot be empty if tool choice is set to a specific tool."
207	            tool_name = request.tool_choice.function.name
208	            tool_exists = any(tool.function.name == tool_name for tool in request.tools)
209	            if not tool_exists:
210	                return f"Tool '{tool_name}' not found in tools list."
211	
212	        # Validate tool definitions
213	        for i, tool in enumerate(request.tools or []):
214	            if tool.function.parameters is None:
215	                continue
216	            try:
217	                Draft202012Validator.check_schema(tool.function.parameters)
218	            except SchemaError as e:
219	                return f"Tool {i} function has invalid 'parameters' schema: {str(e)}"
220	
221	        max_output_tokens = request.max_completion_tokens or request.max_tokens
222	        server_context_length = self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.context_length
223	        if (
224	            max_output_tokens
225	            and server_context_length
226	            and max_output_tokens > server_context_length
227	        ) and not self.tokenizer_manager.server_args.allow_auto_truncate:
228	            return (
229	                f"max_completion_tokens is too large: {max_output_tokens}."
230	                f"This model supports at most {server_context_length} completion tokens."
231	            )
232	
233	        if request.response_format and request.response_format.type == "json_schema":
234	            schema = getattr(request.response_format.json_schema, "schema_", None)
235	            if schema is None:
236	                return "schema_ is required for json_schema response format request."
237	
238	        return None

240	    def _convert_to_internal_request(
241	        self,
242	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
243	        raw_request: Request = None,
244	    ) -> tuple[GenerateReqInput, ChatCompletionRequest]:
245	        reasoning_effort = (
246	            request.chat_template_kwargs.pop("reasoning_effort", None)
247	            if request.chat_template_kwargs
248	            else None
249	        )
250	        if reasoning_effort is not None:
251	            request.reasoning_effort = reasoning_effort
252	
253	        """Convert OpenAI chat completion request to internal format"""
254	        is_multimodal = self.tokenizer_manager.model_config.is_multimodal
255	
256	        # Process messages and apply chat template
257	        processed_messages = self._process_messages(request, is_multimodal)
258	
259	        # Build sampling parameters
260	        sampling_params = request.to_sampling_params(
261	            stop=processed_messages.stop,
262	            model_generation_config=self.default_sampling_params,
263	            tool_call_constraint=processed_messages.tool_call_constraint,
264	        )
265	
266	        # Handle single vs multiple requests
267	        if is_multimodal:
268	            prompt_kwargs = {"text": processed_messages.prompt}
269	        else:
270	            if isinstance(processed_messages.prompt_ids, str):
271	                prompt_kwargs = {"text": processed_messages.prompt_ids}
272	            else:
273	                prompt_kwargs = {"input_ids": processed_messages.prompt_ids}
274	
275	        # Extract custom labels from raw request headers
276	        custom_labels = self.extract_custom_labels(raw_request)
277	
278	        # Resolve LoRA adapter from model parameter or explicit lora_path
279	        lora_path = self._resolve_lora_path(request.model, request.lora_path)
280	        img_max_dynamic_patch, vid_max_dynamic_patch = _extract_max_dynamic_patch(
281	            request
282	        )
283	        adapted_request = GenerateReqInput(
284	            **prompt_kwargs,
285	            image_data=processed_messages.image_data,
286	            video_data=processed_messages.video_data,
287	            audio_data=processed_messages.audio_data,
288	            sampling_params=sampling_params,
289	            return_logprob=request.logprobs,
290	            logprob_start_len=-1,
291	            top_logprobs_num=request.top_logprobs or 0,
292	            stream=request.stream,
293	            return_text_in_logprobs=True,
294	            modalities=processed_messages.modalities,
295	            lora_path=lora_path,
296	            bootstrap_host=request.bootstrap_host,
297	            bootstrap_port=request.bootstrap_port,
298	            bootstrap_room=request.bootstrap_room,
299	            data_parallel_rank=request.data_parallel_rank,
300	            return_hidden_states=request.return_hidden_states,
301	            return_routed_experts=request.return_routed_experts,
302	            rid=request.rid,
303	            extra_key=self._compute_extra_key(request),
304	            require_reasoning=self._get_reasoning_from_request(request),
305	            priority=request.priority,
306	            routing_key=self.extract_routing_key(raw_request),
307	            custom_labels=custom_labels,
308	            custom_logit_processor=request.custom_logit_processor,
309	            image_max_dynamic_patch=img_max_dynamic_patch,
310	            video_max_dynamic_patch=vid_max_dynamic_patch,
311	            max_dynamic_patch=getattr(request, "max_dynamic_patch", None),
312	        )
313	
314	        return adapted_request, request

316	    def _process_messages(
317	        self, request: ChatCompletionRequest, is_multimodal: bool
318	    ) -> MessageProcessingResult:
319	        """Process chat messages and apply chat template"""
320	        # GptOss model needs to keep special tokens for harmony parsing
321	        if self.is_gpt_oss:
322	            request.skip_special_tokens = False
323	
324	        tool_call_constraint = None
325	
326	        # Apply chat template and its stop strings
327	        tools = None
328	        if request.tools and request.tool_choice != "none":
329	            request.skip_special_tokens = False
330	            if not isinstance(request.tool_choice, str):
331	                tools = [
332	                    item.model_dump()
333	                    for item in request.tools
334	                    if item.function.name == request.tool_choice.function.name
335	                ]
336	            else:
337	                tools = [item.model_dump() for item in request.tools]
338	            if self.tool_call_parser:
339	                parser = FunctionCallParser(request.tools, self.tool_call_parser)
340	                tool_call_constraint = parser.get_structure_constraint(
341	                    request.tool_choice
342	                )
343	            # Handle JSON schema constraint directly for required or named tool choice
344	            if request.tool_choice == "required" or isinstance(
345	                request.tool_choice, ToolChoice
346	            ):
347	                json_schema = get_json_schema_constraint(
348	                    request.tools, request.tool_choice
349	                )
350	                tool_call_constraint = ("json_schema", json_schema)
351	
352	        # Use chat template
353	        if self.template_manager.chat_template_name is None:
354	            result = self._apply_jinja_template(request, tools, is_multimodal)
355	        else:
356	            result = self._apply_conversation_template(request, is_multimodal)
357	
358	        result.tool_call_constraint = tool_call_constraint
359	        return result
360	
361	    def _apply_jinja_template(
362	        self,
363	        request: ChatCompletionRequest,
364	        tools: Optional[List[Dict]],
365	        is_multimodal: bool,
366	    ) -> MessageProcessingResult:
367	        """Apply Jinja chat template"""
368	        prompt = ""
369	        prompt_ids = []
370	        openai_compatible_messages = []
371	        image_data = []
372	        video_data = []
373	        audio_data = []
374	        modalities = []
375	
376	        template_content_format = self.template_manager.jinja_template_content_format
377	
378	        if self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding:
379	            thinking_mode = (
380	                "thinking"
381	                if (request.chat_template_kwargs or {}).get("thinking")
382	                else "chat"
383	            )
384	            messages = request.messages
385	            messages = [msg.model_dump() for msg in messages]
386	
387	            for msg in messages:
388	                if msg.get("content") is None:
389	                    msg["content"] = ""
390	                processed_msg = process_content_for_template_format(
391	                    msg,
392	                    template_content_format,
393	                    image_data,
394	                    video_data,
395	                    audio_data,
396	                    modalities,
397	                    use_dpsk_v32_encoding=self.use_dpsk_v32_encoding,
398	                )
399	                msg.update(processed_msg)

12	def _find_common_prefix(s1: str, s2: str) -> str:
13	    prefix = ""
14	    min_length = min(len(s1), len(s2))
15	    for i in range(0, min_length):
16	        if s1[i] == s2[i]:
17	            prefix += s1[i]
18	        else:
19	            break
20	    return prefix

23	def _partial_json_loads(input_str: str, flags: Allow) -> Tuple[Any, int]:
24	    """
25	    Parse incomplete or partial JSON strings commonly encountered during streaming.
26	
27	    Args:
28	        input_str (str): The potentially incomplete JSON string to parse.
29	        flags (Allow): Bitwise flags controlling what types of partial data are allowed.
30	            Common flags include:
31	            - Allow.STR: Allow partial strings (e.g., '"hello wo' -> 'hello wo')
32	            - Allow.OBJ: Allow partial objects (e.g., '{"key":' -> {'key': None})
33	            - Allow.ARR: Allow partial arrays (e.g., '[1, 2,' -> [1, 2])
34	            - Allow.ALL: Allow all types of partial data
35	
36	    Returns:
37	        Tuple[Any, int]: A tuple containing:
38	            - parsed_object: The Python object parsed from the JSON
39	            - consumed_length: Number of characters consumed from input_str
40	    """
41	    try:
42	        return (partial_json_parser.loads(input_str, flags), len(input_str))
43	    except (JSONDecodeError, IndexError) as e:
44	        msg = getattr(e, "msg", str(e))
45	        if "Extra data" in msg or "pop from empty list" in msg:
46	            start = WHITESPACE.match(input_str, 0).end()
47	            obj, end = JSONDecoder().raw_decode(input_str, start)
48	            return obj, end
49	        raise
50

52	def _is_complete_json(input_str: str) -> bool:
53	    try:
54	        orjson.loads(input_str)
55	        return True
56	    except JSONDecodeError:
57	        return False

60	def _get_tool_schema_defs(tools: List[Tool]) -> dict:
61	    """
62	    Get consolidated $defs from all tools, validating for conflicts.
63	
64	    Args:
65	        tools: List of tools to process
66	
67	    Returns:
68	        Dictionary of consolidated $defs from all tools
69	
70	    Raises:
71	        ValueError: If conflicting $defs are found
72	    """
73	    all_defs = {}
74	    for tool in tools:
75	        if tool.function.parameters is None:
76	            continue
77	        defs = tool.function.parameters.get("$defs", {})
78	        for def_name, def_schema in defs.items():
79	            if def_name in all_defs and all_defs[def_name] != def_schema:
80	                raise ValueError(
81	                    f"Tool definition '{def_name}' has "
82	                    "multiple schemas, which is not "
83	                    "supported."
84	                )
85	            else:
86	                all_defs[def_name] = def_schema
87	    return all_defs
88	
89	
90	def _get_tool_schema(tool: Tool) -> dict:
91	    return {
92	        "properties": {
93	            "name": {"type": "string", "enum": [tool.function.name]},
94	            "parameters": (
95	                tool.function.parameters
96	                if tool.function.parameters
97	                else {"type": "object", "properties": {}}
98	            ),
99	        },
100	        "required": ["name", "parameters"],
101	    }
102	
103	
104	def infer_type_from_json_schema(schema: Dict[str, Any]) -> Optional[str]:
105	    """
106	    Infer the primary type of a parameter from JSON Schema.
107	
108	    Supports complex JSON Schema structures including:
109	    - Direct type field (including type arrays)
110	    - anyOf/oneOf: parameter can be any of multiple types
111	    - enum: parameter must be one of enum values
112	    - allOf: parameter must satisfy all type definitions
113	    - properties: inferred as object type
114	    - items: inferred as array type
115	
116	    Args:
117	        schema: JSON Schema definition
118	
119	    Returns:
120	        Inferred type ('string', 'number', 'object', 'array', etc.) or None
121	    """
122	    if not isinstance(schema, dict):
123	        return None
124	
125	    # Priority 1: Direct type field (including type arrays)
126	    if "type" in schema:
127	        type_value = schema["type"]
128	        if isinstance(type_value, str):
129	            return type_value
130	        elif isinstance(type_value, list) and type_value:
131	            # Handle type arrays: return first non-null type
132	            non_null_types = [t for t in type_value if t != "null"]
133	            if non_null_types:
134	                return non_null_types[0]
135	            return "string"  # If only null, default to string
136	
137	    # Priority 2: Handle anyOf/oneOf
138	    if "anyOf" in schema or "oneOf" in schema:
139	        schemas = schema.get("anyOf") or schema.get("oneOf")
140	        types = []
141	
142	        if isinstance(schemas, list):
143	            for sub_schema in schemas:
144	                inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
145	                if inferred_type:
146	                    types.append(inferred_type)
147	
148	            if types:
149	                # If all types are the same, return unified type
150	                if len(set(types)) == 1:
151	                    return types[0]
152	                # When types differ, prioritize string (safest)
153	                if "string" in types:
154	                    return "string"
155	                # Otherwise return first type
156	                return types[0]
157	
158	    # Priority 3: Handle enum (infer type from enum values)
159	    if "enum" in schema and isinstance(schema["enum"], list):
160	        if not schema["enum"]:
161	            return "string"
162	
163	        # Infer type from enum values
164	        enum_types = set()
165	        for value in schema["enum"]:
166	            if value is None:
167	                enum_types.add("null")
168	            elif isinstance(value, bool):
169	                enum_types.add("boolean")
170	            elif isinstance(value, int):
171	                enum_types.add("integer")
172	            elif isinstance(value, float):
173	                enum_types.add("number")
174	            elif isinstance(value, str):
175	                enum_types.add("string")
176	            elif isinstance(value, list):
177	                enum_types.add("array")
178	            elif isinstance(value, dict):
179	                enum_types.add("object")
180	
181	        # If type is uniform, return that type
182	        if len(enum_types) == 1:
183	            return enum_types.pop()
184	        # Mixed types, prioritize string
185	        return "string"
186	
187	    # Priority 4: Handle allOf (must satisfy all types)
188	    if "allOf" in schema and isinstance(schema["allOf"], list):
189	        schemas = schema["allOf"]
190	        for sub_schema in schemas:
191	            inferred_type = infer_type_from_json_schema(sub_schema)
192	            if inferred_type and inferred_type != "string":
193	                return inferred_type
194	        return "string"
195	
196	    # Priority 5: Infer object type
197	    if "properties" in schema:
198	        return "object"
199	
200	    # Priority 6: Infer array type
201	    if "items" in schema:
202	        return "array"
203	
204	    return None
205	
206	
207	def get_json_schema_constraint(
208	    tools: List[Tool], tool_choice: Union[ToolChoice, Literal["required"]]
209	) -> Optional[dict]:
210	    """
211	    Get the JSON schema constraint for the specified tool choice.
212	
213	    Args:
214	        tool_choice: The tool choice specification
215	
216	    Returns:
217	        JSON schema dict, or None if no valid tools found
218	    """
219	
220	    if isinstance(tool_choice, ToolChoice):
221	        # For specific function choice, return the user's parameters schema directly
222	        fn_name = tool_choice.function.name
223	        for tool in tools:
224	            if tool.function.name == fn_name:
225	                return {
226	                    "type": "array",
227	                    "minItems": 1,
228	                    "maxItems": 1,
229	                    "items": _get_tool_schema(tool),
230	                }
231	        return None
232	    elif tool_choice == "required":
233	        json_schema = {
234	            "type": "array",
235	            "minItems": 1,
236	            "items": {
237	                "type": "object",
238	                "anyOf": [_get_tool_schema(tool) for tool in tools],
239	            },
240	        }
241	        json_schema_defs = _get_tool_schema_defs(tools)
242	        if json_schema_defs:
243	            json_schema["$defs"] = json_schema_defs
244	        return json_schema
245	
246	    return None
247

35	def execute_once(func):
36	    has_run = None
37	
38	    @wraps(func)
39	    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
40	        nonlocal has_run
41	        if not has_run:
42	            func(*args, **kwargs)
43	            has_run = True
44	
45	    return wrapper

53	def convert_json_schema_to_str(json_schema: Union[dict, str, Type[BaseModel]]) -> str:
54	    """Convert a JSON schema to a string.
55	    Parameters
56	    ----------
57	    json_schema
58	        The JSON schema.
59	    Returns
60	    -------
61	    str
62	        The JSON schema converted to a string.
63	    Raises
64	    ------
65	    ValueError
66	        If the schema is not a dictionary, a string or a Pydantic class.
67	    """
68	    if isinstance(json_schema, dict):
69	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema)
70	    elif isinstance(json_schema, str):
71	        schema_str = json_schema
72	    elif issubclass(json_schema, BaseModel):
73	        schema_str = json.dumps(json_schema.model_json_schema())
74	    else:
75	        raise ValueError(
76	            f"Cannot parse schema {json_schema}. The schema must be either "
77	            + "a Pydantic class, a dictionary or a string that contains the JSON "
78	            + "schema specification"
79	        )
80	    return schema_str

1	from abc import ABC, abstractmethod
2	from dataclasses import dataclass
3	from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
4	
5	import numpy as np
6	
7	
8	@dataclass
9	class ChoicesDecision:
10	    decision: str
11	    meta_info: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None
12	
13	
14	class ChoicesSamplingMethod(ABC):
15	
16	    @property
17	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
18	        return False
19	
20	    @abstractmethod
21	    def __call__(
22	        self,
23	        *,
24	        choices: List[str],
25	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
26	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
27	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
28	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
29	    ) -> ChoicesDecision: ...
30	
31	
32	class TokenLengthNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
33	
34	    def __call__(
35	        self,
36	        *,
37	        choices: List[str],
38	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
39	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
40	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
41	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
42	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
43	        """Select the option with the highest token length normalized prompt logprob."""
44	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_prompt_logprobs)]
45	        meta_info = {
46	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
47	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
48	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
49	        }
50	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
51	
52	
53	token_length_normalized = TokenLengthNormalized()
54	
55	
56	class GreedyTokenSelection(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
57	
58	    def __call__(
59	        self,
60	        *,
61	        choices: List[str],
62	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
63	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
64	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
65	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
66	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
67	        """Select the option based on greedy logprob selection. For overlapping options
68	        where one option is a subset of a longer option, extend the shorter option using
69	        its average logprob for comparison against the longer option."""
70	
71	        num_options = len(choices)
72	        max_tokens = max(len(option) for option in input_token_logprobs)
73	        logprob_matrix = self._build_logprob_matrix(
74	            input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options
75	        )
76	        remaining = self._greedy_selection(logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens)
77	
78	        best_choice = choices[remaining[0]]
79	        meta_info = {
80	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
81	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
82	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
83	            "greedy_logprob_matrix": logprob_matrix.tolist(),
84	        }
85	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
86	
87	    def _build_logprob_matrix(self, input_token_logprobs, max_tokens, num_options):
88	        logprob_matrix = np.zeros((num_options, max_tokens))
89	        for i, option in enumerate(input_token_logprobs):
90	            actual_logprobs = [token[0] for token in option]
91	            avg_logprob = np.mean(actual_logprobs)
92	            logprob_matrix[i, : len(option)] = actual_logprobs
93	            if len(option) < max_tokens:
94	                logprob_matrix[i, len(option) :] = avg_logprob
95	        return logprob_matrix
96	
97	    def _greedy_selection(self, logprob_matrix, num_options, max_tokens):
98	        remaining = np.arange(num_options)
99	        for j in range(max_tokens):
100	            max_logprob = np.max(logprob_matrix[remaining, j])
101	            remaining = remaining[logprob_matrix[remaining, j] == max_logprob]
102	            if len(remaining) == 1:
103	                break
104	        return remaining

110	class UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized(ChoicesSamplingMethod):
111	
112	    @property
113	    def requires_unconditional_logprobs(self) -> bool:
114	        return True
115	
116	    def __call__(
117	        self,
118	        *,
119	        choices: List[str],
120	        normalized_prompt_logprobs: List[float],
121	        input_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
122	        output_token_logprobs: List[List[Any]],
123	        unconditional_token_logprobs: Optional[List[List[Any]]] = None,
124	    ) -> ChoicesDecision:
125	        """Select the option with the highest average token logprob once normalized by
126	        the unconditional token logprobs.
127	
128	        The first unconditional token logprob is assumed to be None. If so, it is
129	        replaced with 0 for the purposes of normalization."""
130	
131	        if unconditional_token_logprobs is None:
132	            raise ValueError(
133	                "Unconditional token logprobs are required for this method."
134	            )
135	
136	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = self._normalize_logprobs(
137	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
138	        )
139	
140	        best_choice = choices[np.argmax(normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs)]
141	        meta_info = {
142	            "normalized_prompt_logprobs": normalized_prompt_logprobs,
143	            "input_token_logprobs": input_token_logprobs,
144	            "output_token_logprobs": output_token_logprobs,
145	            "unconditional_token_logprobs": unconditional_token_logprobs,
146	            "normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs": normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs,
147	        }
148	        return ChoicesDecision(decision=best_choice, meta_info=meta_info)
149	
150	    def _normalize_logprobs(self, input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs):
151	        normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs = []
152	        for inputs, unconditionals in zip(
153	            input_token_logprobs, unconditional_token_logprobs
154	        ):
155	            inputs_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in inputs])
156	            unconditionals_logprobs = np.array([token[0] for token in unconditionals])
157	            unconditionals_logprobs[0] = unconditionals_logprobs[0] or 0
158	            normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs.append(
159	                float(np.mean(inputs_logprobs - unconditionals_logprobs))
160	            )
161	        return normalized_unconditional_prompt_logprobs
162	
163	
164	unconditional_likelihood_normalized = UnconditionalLikelihoodNormalized()

17	
18	import torch
19	
20	from .base_grammar_backend import (
21	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
22	    BaseGrammarBackend,
23	    BaseGrammarObject,
24	)
